payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the March 2025 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees, containing service performance, ridership, financial results, major projects, customer and community, and safety and security data for both LIRR and Metro-North.

payload.description: Renovation bringing the Mastic-Shirley Station to a state of good repair, beautifying and modernizing it. Includes a new sheltered 2,000-square-foot plaza area featuring historic photos, a reconstructed platform shelter, new LED lighting, railings, signage, platform and ADA ramp repairs, digital information screen, and an upgraded security and communication system.

Replacing and upgrading the Jamaica Substation originally constructed in 1971. Located west of Jamaica Station in Richmond Hill, Queens (corner of 132nd Street and 91st Ave). A new three-story prefabricated modular substation building will be constructed. Scope includes design, fabrication, and installation of modular sections housing AC switchgear, rectifiers, DC switchgear, control cabinets, a motor generator and associated equipment. The existing substation will be decommissioned and demolished.
payload.project_type: state of good repair / substation replacement

payload.description: Metro-North debuted Locomotive No. 222, the sixth locomotive in the Heritage Locomotive Series, with an inaugural run from Danbury, CT, to Grand Central Terminal on February 28, 2025. Pays tribute to the historic New York, New Haven and Hartford Railroad with olive green and gold color scheme from the 1940-50s. Restoration involved teams from Harmon Diesel Shop, Stamford Heavy Repair, and North White Plains Freight and Paint Shops.

Successful implementation of significant run-time improvements on the New Haven Line through collaboration with CTDOT and targeted infrastructure upgrades including signal system enhancements, optimized train routing, and limited station stops during peak periods. Six CTDOT Super Express trains now provide faster service between New Haven and Grand Central Terminal, reducing travel times by as much as 10 minutes.

tool_args.label: Upper Hudson and Upper Harlem Station Repairs

payload.project_name: Station Upgrades Along the Hudson and Harlem Lines
payload.description: Comprehensive station repair project making priority repairs to five key stations: New Hamburg, Beacon, Cold Spring, Garrison, and Southeast. Repairs include platform replacement, concrete pier repair, stair repairs, railing installation, tactile warning strip installation, edge board installation, and painting.

warnings: project.partner_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency is relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

payload.description: Metro-North debuted Locomotive No. 222, the latest addition to the Heritage Locomotive Series, with an inaugural run from Danbury, CT, to Grand Central Terminal.
Metro-North schedule adjustments on the Hudson, Harlem, and New Haven Lines to advance critical infrastructure improvements and essential maintenance work.

tool_args.label: National Transit Employee Appreciation Day at Penn Station

payload.description: Special event at Penn Station with MTA Chairman and transit agency presidents in recognition of employees' efforts.

tool_args.label: Big East Tournament Kickoff at Penn Station

payload.event_name: Big East Tournament Kickoff featuring St. John's Red Storm
payload.description: Train ride from Jamaica to Penn Station with Athletic Director Ed Kull, school band, cheerleaders, mascot Johnny Thunderbird and LIRR President Rob Free. Pep rally at Penn Station with Chairman Lieber, music, speeches. Station lights illuminated in red and white.
